nodes.context: A gang of primarily Turkish immigrants from Berlin-Kreuzberg, active from late 1980s to mid-1990s, involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, burglary, happy slapping, and vandalism.
A subgroup of the 36 Boys with higher potential of violence.
Rival group to the 36 Boys, involved in turf battles.
Rival group to the 36 Boys, involved in turf battles.
Group that the 36 Boys initially joined during May riots, but alliance was short-lived.

edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the 36 Boys there were the "36 Juniors", who showed a higher potential of violence.
edges.context: 36 Juniors are a subgroup of the 36 Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: In the beginning of the 1990s the 36 Boys fought against turf battles with Nazis and Skinheads in the Jungfernheide park.
edges.context: 36 Boys fought turf battles with Nazis.

edges.evidence_quote: In the beginning of the 1990s the 36 Boys fought against turf battles with Nazis and Skinheads in the Jungfernheide park.
edges.context: 36 Boys fought turf battles with Skinheads.

edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs were the Warriors from the Schlesisches Tor and the Black Panthers from Wedding.
edges.context: Warriors are a rival gang to the 36 Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs were the Warriors from the Schlesisches Tor and the Black Panthers from Wedding.
edges.context: Black Panthers are a rival gang to the 36 Boys.

edges.evidence_quote: During the May riots in Kreuzberg, the 36 Boys initially joined the Autonomists, but the alliance was short-lived because of the group's lack of political orientation.
edges.context: 36 Boys initially allied with Autonomists during May riots, but alliance was short-lived.
